item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The moment the SCP considers this option and confirms it, it is gonna be a field day for the memetic agencies and related departments for how much this has gone under the radar.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Somewhat pedantic issue I have with this:

Shouldn't the Emperor/Terracotta Soldiers be speaking in Old Chinese, rather than Standard Chinese, which postdates them by about 2200 years?

Still like the concept, even if it's been tackled in mainstream media in a similar fashion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Emperor Huangdi's

I'm nitpicking here, but it should be 'Emperor Shi' or 'Shi Huangdi' or 'Qin SHihuang' (the most conventional term in English). 'Huangdi' is the title 'Emperor'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's a particularly chilling thought I had regarding this SCP.

Although Emperor Huangdi's spiritual powers are never described, perhaps he can corrupt people, in a way-especially all its future leaders.

Think about it. He lies underneath the very soil of China itself. While he can't fully escape, he can spread and corrupt those in power by increasing their greed and ruthlessness.

This gets heartbreaking when you consider modern China. The country wanted to modernize, become strong as the Western powers, take a future role in defining the world. Maybe Dr. Sun was strong enough to resist the Emperor's influence, and in revenge, the Emperor made Yuan Shikai President instead. Yuan quickly succumbed to the Emperor's poisonous words.

Things got worse. Chiang Kai-shek fell under the Emperor's spell, started the civil war and became an authoritarian military dictator, becoming increasingly brutal until he established martial law in Taiwan. Mao Zedong? He wanted to help the peasants at first, but he gave in so deeply that he nearly tore China apart with famine and the Cultural revolution, killing off former comrades in the name of preserving his power. And it goes on, and on, and on.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Wikiwalk Crosslink Added as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Got a few potential links that could work for this article in terms of facilitating the wikiwalk. The first is a link to SCP-2847 which mentions SCP-2101 as a site of potential interest for GoI-004X (Xia Anomalous Culture Group). The second is a link to SCP-926, a musical instrument that allows for one to control terracotta warriors, and something you'd likely want far from 2101 if your plans involve you siding with the soldiers. As such, I am recommending the following edit:

Changing this:

Communication with SCP-2101-1 must be approved in a request form beforehand, to avoid unnecessary strain.

To this:

Communication with SCP-2101-1 must be approved in a request form beforehand, to avoid unnecessary strain. At no point is SCP-926 or similar anomalies to be allowed within SCP-2101.

Changing this:

SCP-2101 refers to a sphere of effect located at 34.3849° N, 109.2731° E, the location of the Mausoleum of the First Qin Emperor. (秦始皇陵及兵马俑坑) (transliteration: Qín shǐ huáng líng jí bīng mǎ yǒng kēng) The origin point of SCP-2101 is located roughly 8 meters below the current lowest level of the mausoleum.

To this:

SCP-2101 refers to a sphere of effect located at 34.3849° N, 109.2731° E, the location of the Mausoleum of the First Qin Emperor. (秦始皇陵及兵马俑坑) (transliteration: Qín shǐ huáng líng jí bīng mǎ yǒng kēng) The origin point of SCP-2101 is located roughly 8 meters below the current lowest level of the mausoleum1.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

Footnotes
1. The Masoleum is currently believed to be a location of interest for the Xia Anomalous Culture Group (GoI-004X), see SCP-2847 for more information

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 7 to 6, good to know we're that close to being screwed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know guys, it's not that implausible for a Chinese emperor to offer huge tracts of land to people who might aid him. Downvoting from Monty Python seems so petty to me.

Regarding the article: it's very well done, in my opinion. Upvoted. I really like SCP's with foundations on history and myth.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, that bugged me. But in the end, I think the execution is good enough to see past that, even if I saw the twist coming from far.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-CN had seen so many SCPs circling around Shi Huang Ti, we basically canonized him as the ancient Chinese anomaly-exploiter emperor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I once recalled a Martin Mystery episode based on the premises that the Terracotta Army guards Qin Shi Huang as his jailor. While that concept is not all that original, it is better than playing it straight, I suppose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I tend to agree with that… it almost made me skip reading the rest of the skip (and this is coming from someone who has a Monty Python reference in one of his *own* skips). I liked the rest though so an upvote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Monty Python reference really took me out of it. -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: bing ma yong keng is not Mausoleum of the First Emperor. bing ma yong keng is name of shafts containing terracotta army. bing ma yong keng is apart from Mausoleum itself about ~1 km.

Fuck Fuck this is epic. I love those historical-mythical skips.

Edit: Just one thing bothers me. Why would 秦始皇 be speaking a language modern people could understand easily? And I'm sure it isn't Mandarin.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This one is… very good. I think it was the soldiers containing the Emperor that got my upvote.

The sacrifice itself is enormous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aren't there many of these terracotta warrior 兵马俑already? Unoriginal!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Traditional is used in Taiwan (where I self-identify) but simplified is used in the mainland. Given that this SCP is located in the mainland, I decided to go with simplified.

(Also, I'm better with simplified than I am with traditional.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dunno, but I think using traditional Chinese is better suited for an document like this, as traditional is used more than simplified.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I also snickered, but I must say it really broke the immersion. I'd strongly recommend you change it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: See SCP-926 for a different take on a similar concept. This makes three different SCP articles with animated versions of the terracotta army.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, good. I'm not the only one who snickered at that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would like this more if its story wasn't overlaid on a historic site. I can't help but think too many people have been there and generally know about the terracotta soldiers for this to be a believable anomaly.

Either way, you should nix "huge tracts of land", as all I could think after reading it was a Monty Python sketch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice take on the terracotta army. It has an old-style vibe to it. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes give me your upvotes ihpkmn.

It's the only way to survive

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What's this? The Foundation working with the anomaly to help contain a greater danger?

UNACCEPTABLE. Truly, the only way to counter this is to upvote it and hope it goes away. (Upvotes send it into the negatives, right? Ah well!)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
:P

Hope you enjoyed more than just that reference.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Huge tracts of land.

ehehehehehehe

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Touche… now my imagination is going crazy thinking of all the things that SCP-2101 could be if 2101-1 was lying…

I feel obligated to up-vote this not just because it was able to stir up my imagination, but also because it is a pretty interesting idea and a good take on the terracotta army. Nicely done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Who's to say that 2101-1 is telling the truth?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This might be a little off-topic, but why did six O5's vote against aiding the SCP-2101-1's in containing SCP-2101? That kind of sounds hypocritical of the foundation imho.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hurf. That was silly of me. Thanks for catching the little typos.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
[Seismic activity begins. A new SCP-2101-1 instance (designated SCP-2101-2) steps forward]

Sorry, I guess this was also a typo.

Alright, I'm upvoting. Thinking on it, this idea's just pretty darn good. And I like the recovery report. Typos are just a pet peeve of mine, I guess. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a threat response thing. It grabbed an agent and wouldn't let go, so they had to help free the guy. You also have to keep in mind that this os 1974 Foundation, not modern Foundation. With a smaller size and less resources, there's only so much that they can do.

I'm not quite sure what that second thing is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for correcting it, as yes, it was in error. I'm not quite sure what you mean by deciding whether or not I should call the second one SCP-2101-1-2 or not, since that's the only thing that I call him by.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seems interesting, but there are some clear issues. For example, in the recovery report, you need to decide whether the second instance of SCP-2101-1 is referred to as SCP-2101-2 or SCP-2101-1-2. And at one point, you appear to have gotten the designation for SCP-2101 itself incorrect. (It was listed as SCP-2101-1 despite clearly being SCP-2101. If this was intentional, and I edited it wrongly, then I apologize sincerely.)

It's a neat enough idea, but there's some obvious mistakes in designation that you need to sort out. Neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have certain issues with this particular piece.

1: I feel the interview is not really in-character (specially when the Foundation destroyed anomalous terracota statues because one of them grabbed the agent's hand and yelled).

2: Man, this is pretty much one of Martin Mistery's episodes step by step.

Sorry, but I don't like it at all.

item.title: Author Overview
item.content:encoded:
The inspiration for this one came from a deleted coldpost that I saw a week or two ago called the "Terracotta Army." The original had nothing to do with the terracotta army, but my brain ran with the idea, and this is what happened.

Thanks to Zolgamax, TheInfinityArch, TwistedGears, and Djoric for reading this in chat. Title credit goes to Zolgamax.
